items.headline: S&P 500’s Strong May Footing Powered by Apple, SanDisk, and Easing Oil
items.summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) clinched 7,200 for the first time ever yesterday, capping its best month since November 2020, and the index is set to open May trading on a high note. Apple’s blowout earnings, a memory chip windfall at SanDisk, and falling oil combine into one of the friendliest setups stocks have seen all ...
